name: Llama 3 8B
summary: Llama 3 8B is the instruction-tuned 8-billion parameter variant of Meta's Llama 3 family, offering the best efficiency-to-performance ratio for consumer GPU deployment. It outperforms the previous Llama 2 13B and 70B models on many reasoning and coding benchmarks despite its smaller size.
use_when: On-device or consumer GPU chat applications requiring strong reasoning and instruction-following. Fine-tuning on custom datasets with limited compute (8GB-24GB VRAM). Multi-turn conversational agents where inference cost per token must be minimized.
avoid_when: Tasks requiring extreme long-context reasoning beyond 8K tokens. Production systems where sub-100ms token latency is required without quantization. Use cases where the larger Llama 3 70B or 405B variants would meaningfully improve accuracy.
pros: Superior efficiency-to-performance ratio in the Llama 3 family, outperforming Llama 2 13B on most benchmarks at half the parameter count
Excellent instruction-following and conversational quality after supervised fine-tuning and RLHF on diverse multilingual data
Broad ecosystem support including quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF), LoRA fine-tuning, and vLLM inference server compatibility
8K token context window with strong performance on retrieval-augmented generation and document question-answering tasks
cons: Requires 16GB+ VRAM for full-precision inference or careful quantization to run on consumer hardware
Struggles with complex mathematical reasoning and long-horizon planning compared to frontier models like GPT-4 and Claude 3
Llama 3 tokenizer uses a byte-fallback strategy that can produce fragmented outputs for low-resource languages and rare Unicode characters
Meta's commercial license has usage restrictions for organizations exceeding 700 million monthly active users

key_hyperparams.name: temperature
key_hyperparams.note: Sampling temperature for generation. Lower to 0.1-0.3 for deterministic code and factual answers. Increase to 0.8-1.0 for creative writing and brainstorming.

key_hyperparams.name: top_p
key_hyperparams.note: Nucleus sampling threshold. Set to 0.8-0.9 for balanced diversity. Lower to 0.5 for more focused outputs. Top-p 1.0 disables nucleus filtering.

key_hyperparams.name: max_new_tokens
key_hyperparams.note: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Increase to 2048-4096 for long-form content generation. Balance with context window to avoid truncation.

key_hyperparams.name: repetition_penalty
key_hyperparams.note: Penalty factor for repeated tokens. Increase to 1.1-1.2 to reduce repetitive phrases in chat and creative writing. 1.0 disables the penalty.

quick_start:
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
    'meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct',
    torch_dtype='auto',
    device_map='auto'
)
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct')

messages = [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Explain quantum computing'}]
input_text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False)
inputs = tokenizer(input_text, return_tensors='pt').to(model.device)
outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=256, temperature=0.6)
